mnemonics: Think of a blockchain as a digital LEGO tower. Each new block (project milestone) is securely attached to the one below it. Tampering with one block (a previous requirement) would make the whole tower (your project) unstable and obvious to everyone.
keyPoints: Clearly define the problem your project aims to solve using blockchain.
Choose the appropriate consensus mechanism (e.g., Proof of Work, Proof of Stake) for your project's needs.
Thoroughly map and document the smart contract logic and data flow.
commonMistakes: Using blockchain where a simple centralized database would be more efficient (i.e., using a sledgehammer to crack a nut).
Neglecting gas optimization and cost considerations when writing smart contracts, leading to prohibitively expensive transactions.
